behaviors.description: The keyword-frequency analyzer assigns the correct sentiment when the review contains only words from the configured positive and negative word lists.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in local counting/threshold logic, not a module handoff. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP: none. Rejection-gate: Integration was rejected because behavior text is limited to the analyzer’s own scoring rule with configured lists, which can be supplied directly in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Call the keyword-frequency analyzer function/object with injected positive/negative lists and thresholds; assert sentiment for positive-only, negative-only, and neutral edge inputs where only configured keywords appear.

behaviors.description: The explanation analyzer produces reasoning that references specific phrases from the original review, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on generation quality/content (specific phrase grounding vs boilerplate), which stubs can hide. MOCK-HIDE: mocked explainer would always pass phrase-reference checks if hardcoded. MULTI-STEP: single generation step, so not Workflow. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because behavior text describes one analyzer output, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real explanation analyzer on a fixed review set and assert each explanation contains concrete references to source phrases (e.g., quoted or near-exact spans) plus low overlap with known boilerplate templates.

behaviors.description: When a review contains mixed signals (both positive and negative keywords), the sentiment score reflects the configured tie-breaking rule.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is deterministic tie-break logic inside the sentiment analyzer. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP: none. Rejection-gate: Integration rejected because behavior text focuses on configured tie-breaking in scoring logic, testable by directly passing config and input to one local component.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the sentiment analyzer with mixed-keyword reviews that produce equal/near-equal counts; vary tie-break configuration and assert selected sentiment matches each configured rule.

behaviors.description: The explanation for a sarcastic review (e.g., "Great, another broken feature") correctly identifies the negative intent despite positive surface words.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: sarcasm/intent recognition is model judgment quality, not deterministic wrapper logic. MOCK-HIDE: a stubbed explainer can be forced correct and miss true sarcasm failures. MULTI-STEP: single model decision, not multi-agent pipeline. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because no sequential agentic chain is specified in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate the real explanation analyzer on a sarcasm-focused dataset; assert the explanation explicitly indicates negative intent and cites sarcastic cues rather than literal positive wording.

behaviors.description: Both outputs (score and explanation) are returned together in a single response, with the explanation referencing the same sentiment direction as the score.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is orchestration + semantic consistency across deterministic scoring and generated explanation. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: although module handoff exists, correctness still depends on real explanation content matching sentiment direction (LLM-DEP yes), so Integration is too low. MULTI-STEP: only one agentic generation step, so not Workflow. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because behavior text does not require multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Call the full processing endpoint with real explanation generation enabled; assert response includes both fields in one payload and that explanation sentiment direction (derived via rubric/labeler) matches the returned score for a mixed input suite.
